pi_items.source: Direct URL verification: HTTP HEAD request returned 200 OK; file downloaded and verified
Confirmed as correct filename in Natural Earth 110m populated places zip archive contents
File path consistent with task description and export script (export_result.sh)
DBSCAN documentation confirms min_cluster_size/MinPts of 3 is a valid parameter; consistent with DBSCAN algorithm
DBSCAN documentation confirms epsilon=5 degrees is valid for geographic spatial clustering; reasonable for global city dataset spanning ~360 degrees longitude
Direct analysis of ne_110m_populated_places.shp file: parsed shapefile header and record structure; feature count extracted from SHP file format (file_length=6904 bytes, record_count=243)
Natural Earth version extracted from ne_110m_populated_places.VERSION.txt in downloaded zip archive
Confirmed from Natural Earth CDN (naciscdn.org) URL and dataset naming convention; official Natural Earth dataset
Confirmed from URL path structure and natural earth dataset naming: ne_110m_* indicates 1:110m scale
Confirmed from zip archive contents: contains .shp, .dbf, .shx, .prj, .cpg files (standard shapefile format)
DBF file analysis: parsed field descriptors from ne_110m_populated_places.dbf header
DBF file field listing includes 'NAME' field (type C, length 100) and multiple NAME_* variants for different languages

privileged_info_summary: The task uses Natural Earth 110m Populated Places dataset (version 5.1.2), which contains exactly 243 point features representing major world cities. The dataset is downloaded from the official Natural Earth CDN (https://naciscdn.org/naturalearth/110m/cultural/ne_110m_populated_places.zip) as a vector shapefile with 137 attributes including NAME fields. The task applies DBSCAN clustering with epsilon=5 degrees and min_cluster_size=3 parameters, which are standard choices for spatial clustering of geographic points. The data is real, publicly available, and the output is expected to be exported as GeoJSON format to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/clustered_cities.geojson.
